skill.name: AC-Nexus
skill.description: AI Agent smart AC control library supporting 17+ AC brands via Broadlink IR and Xiaomi MIoT. Zero GUI dependency, import and use.
skill.summary: Cross-platform AC control library for Broadlink RM IR blasters and Xiaomi MIoT-compatible IR remote controllers, designed for AI agents.
skill.author: oywq00008-cell

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 153 potential issues but evaluation confirms these are false positives. The skill is a legitimate home automation library for controlling air conditioners. The 'shell backtick execution' findings are markdown code blocks in documentation, not actual code execution. Hardcoded IPs are private addresses (192.168.x.x) used as examples. The filesystem and credential references are standard IoT patterns for storing device tokens and config. The risk is rated LOW due to documented persistence behaviors (background scheduler daemon) that warrant user awareness.

high_findings.title: Persistent background scheduler daemon
high_findings.description: The skill starts a background scheduler thread during init() that persists beyond agent task completion. Scheduled on/off times, auto-adjust, and storm auto-shutdown will continue running autonomously. This is documented and expected behavior for home automation, but users should be aware that enabling schedules creates persistent autonomous behavior.

medium_findings.title: Configuration writes to user filesystem
medium_findings.description: The skill writes config files to ~/.ac_controller/ including API keys, device tokens, and schedule templates. Users should review what data is stored in this directory. This is standard for IoT tools but represents credential storage on disk.

medium_findings.title: Local network device control
medium_findings.description: The skill communicates with devices on the local network (192.168.x.x range) including Broadlink IR blasters and Xiaomi MIoT devices. Device tokens are stored and used for network authentication. This is expected for home automation but represents network access patterns.

low_findings.title: Documentation contains extensive code examples
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md file contains 100+ code blocks showing usage examples. Static analysis flagged these as 'shell backtick execution' patterns, but they are markdown code fences, not actual shell execution. All examples are safe demonstration code.

low_findings.title: External API dependencies for weather data
low_findings.description: The skill uses Baidu Weather API and QWeather API for weather data, and National Hurricane Center / China NMC for storm tracking. These are legitimate third-party APIs documented in the skill description.

content.user_title: Control Smart Air Conditioners with AI Agents
content.value_statement: Managing multiple air conditioner brands requires separate apps and manual remote control. AC-Nexus provides a unified Python API for AI agents to control Broadlink IR and Xiaomi MIoT devices programmatically, enabling automated scheduling and smart climate control.
actual_capabilities: Controls 17+ AC brands including Gree, Midea, Hisense, Daikin, Mitsubishi, Hitachi, Fujitsu, and more
Connects to Broadlink RM series IR blasters via local network
Supports Xiaomi MIoT-compatible IR remote controllers with auto-matched siid/piid
Provides weather data from dual sources: Baidu Weather and QWeather
Tracks storms from China NMC and US National Hurricane Center
Runs background scheduler for automated on/off cycles and storm auto-shutdown
limitations: IR learning only works with Broadlink devices, not Xiaomi MIoT devices
Requires Python 3.9 or later and network connectivity to devices
Device tokens must be obtained through Xiaomi QR code login for MIoT support
Storm auto-shutdown requires internet access for weather data fetching

use_cases.title: Automated home climate control
use_cases.description: Set up scheduled on/off times for air conditioners based on daily routines, with automatic temperature adjustment based on outdoor weather conditions.

use_cases.title: Multi-brand AC unification
use_cases.description: Control air conditioners from different brands (Gree, Midea, Hisense) through a single Python API instead of juggling multiple manufacturer apps.
use_cases.target_user: Property managers with diverse AC brands

use_cases.title: Storm-aware AC protection
use_cases.description: Automatically shut down air conditioners when severe storms approach, preventing damage from power fluctuations and reducing energy waste.

prompt_templates.title: Initialize AC control
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up AC-Nexus to control my Gree air conditioner. I have a Broadlink RM4 mini on my network. Guide me through the initialization process.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time setup with Broadlink IR blaster

prompt_templates.title: Add Xiaomi MIoT device
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a Xiaomi IR remote controller connected to my Mi Home app. Show me how to add it to AC-Nexus using QR login and start controlling it via local network.

prompt_templates.title: Create weekday schedule
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a schedule that turns on my living room AC at 8:00 AM and off at 6:00 PM on weekdays (Monday-Friday), with different hours for weekends.

prompt_templates.title: Storm protection automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure AC-Nexus to automatically shut down all air conditioners if a typhoon comes within 100km of my location, and resume normal operation after the storm passes.

output_examples.input: Is there a storm near my location?
output_examples.output: Nearest storm: Typhoon Mawar at 85km. Wind speed: 45 m/s. AC shutdown triggered for all devices. Scheduler paused until storm passes.

output_examples.input: Turn on the bedroom AC to 25°C in cool mode
output_examples.output: Command sent to 二楼卧室 (Broadlink e870723f41ee). AC powered on, mode: cool, temperature: 25°C, fan: auto. IR signal transmitted successfully.

[broadlink] 客厅 = e870723f41ee
[xiaomi_cloud] 主卧 = 2003509235
[xiaomi_cloud] 书房 = 2003509236

Total: 3 devices across 2 providers

best_practices: Always confirm with the user before enabling schedule templates or auto-adjust features, as these create persistent background behavior
Use meaningful device names (room/location) instead of MAC addresses for easier agent intent matching
Set schedule_enabled=False and auto_adjust=False when disabling automation to prevent background execution
Store device tokens securely and review ~/.ac_controller/config.json periodically for sensitive data
anti_patterns: Do not enable auto-adjust or typhoon auto-shutdown without explicit user consent for persistent autonomous operation
Do not commit ~/.ac_controller/config.json to version control as it contains API keys and device tokens
Do not hardcode device tokens or API keys in agent code; use the config system instead

faq.question: What AC brands does AC-Nexus support?
faq.answer: AC-Nexus supports 17+ brands including Gree, Midea, Hisense, Daikin, Mitsubishi, Hitachi, Fujitsu, Ballu, Carrier, Hyundai, Fuego, Haier, AUX, and Panasonic, plus all Xiaomi MIoT-compatible IR remote controllers.

faq.question: Do I need a Broadlink device to use AC-Nexus?
faq.answer: No, you can use Xiaomi MIoT-compatible IR remote controllers with AC-Nexus without a Broadlink device. Broadlink is required only for IR learning and brands not in the built-in protocol library.

faq.question: How does the storm auto-shutdown work?
faq.answer: AC-Nexus fetches typhoon data from China NMC or US NHC, calculates distance to your location, and triggers shutdown based on wind speed and distance thresholds. The scheduler is automatically paused during severe weather.

faq.question: Is my data sent to external servers?
faq.answer: AC control commands are sent locally to your devices on the local network. Weather and storm data are fetched from Baidu/QWeather and NMC/NHC APIs as documented. Device tokens and API keys are stored locally in ~/.ac_controller/config.json.

faq.question: Can I disable the background scheduler?
faq.answer: Yes, set schedule_enabled=False and auto_adjust=False for each device, then call _cfg.save_config(_cfg.config). The scheduler will not start autonomous actions when these flags are disabled.

faq.question: What platforms are supported?
faq.answer: AC-Nexus runs on any device with Python 3.9 or later, including macOS, Windows, Linux, Raspberry Pi, NAS devices, and OpenWRT routers. Pre-built GUI installers are available for desktop platforms.
